POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make the agentic outline top-benchmark: it must COMPUTE-and-PROVE numbers (the moat frontier products lack), process the FULL 346-basket corpus with cp4_used=agentic (never fallback-plain; seed-fallback only on GLM-5.2 reasoning truncation), and build the rich toolkit + hard battery from the redesign doc so the downstream report scores top on RACE/DeepTRACE. Read /workspace/POLARIS/docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md (read-only) for the binding T1/T2 spec.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: (a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch bot/outline-agent-box). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE OPUS — the hands AND the eyes. Do the hard work yourself: read the exact files named, search
where needed, build, fix, run, MEASURE. Do not wait for anyone to read the code for you.

CURRENT STATE (accurate as of wheel start — verify, don't assume):
worktree /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent @ HEAD 98726ca (branch bot/outline-agent-box). Inherited work is SOLID and PASSES: 19 tests green in ~2.8s (tests/polaris_graph/outline/test_outline_agent_w3.py + tests/polaris_graph/tools/test_wrap_execute_python.py). execute_python is un-bricked (client gated on _CODEGEN_TOOLS). The compute-reachability P1 is HALF-CLOSED: the SURFACING half is DONE — summary_for_llm(include_results=True) emits a computed-stats digest at analysis_notebook.py:227-276, so the decide LLM can now SEE computed values. The RENDER half is STILL OPEN at outline_agent.py:1609 — a computed number cannot yet render through the verified [#calc:] lane. NOT BUILT YET: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py does not exist; all 9 non-compute T1 tools (fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence, find_contradictions, corroboration_profile) are missing; scripts/outline_battery.py does not exist; the fold-in seam (_offset_renumber outline_agent.py:610 + _stamp_and_delete :792) is NOT yet factored as a reusable unit, which blocks fetch_url. Only WHEEL_PROGRESS.md is uncommitted (a placeholder — fine).

YOUR ACTIONS THIS ROUND (precise; do these, in order, then MEASURE):
STEP 1 (close the render-half P1, highest correctness value): trace outline_agent.py:1609 and the [#calc:] lane at quantified_analysis.py:617; make a computed number render through [#calc:] end-to-end on ONE real case, and PROVE with a test that a derived number routed to [CITE:ev_xxx] is still DROPPED by strict_verify check (d) — i.e. the moat works AND the faithfulness gate holds. Commit. STEP 2 (factor the fold-in seam): extract _offset_renumber(:610)+_stamp_and_delete(:792) into a shared reusable unit imported back into outline_agent.py, with a test asserting id-collision safety — this unblocks fetch_url. Commit. STEP 3 (build T1 outline_toolkit.py): register_outline_toolkit() wiring the 9 tools + verified_compute per redesign doc PART 4; add per-tool unit tests. Commit incrementally, one tool group at a time — never a big unverified batch. Measure: tests pass + tool count. Do STEP 1 before anything else; it is the correctness moat.

RULES:
- Actually EXERCISE every change (run it; a heartbeat/concurrency probe, a real test, a real score —
  py_compile is NOT proof). Commit what passes.
- MEASURE the success metric ((a) A computed number (e.g. a delta/ratio) can be COMPUTED by the agent AND RENDER in the outline via the verified [#calc:] lane (quantified_analysis.py:617), never via [CITE:ev_xxx] — proven end-to-end on one real case; (b) the T1 toolkit tools land and pass real unit tests; (c) the T2 battery runs and S0/S1 failures trend to zero. Faithfulness invariant is the hard gate: NO derived/unverified number may ever reach the [CITE:ev_xxx] render path (that would be a P0 breach).) with a real number this round if at all possible.
- End by writing ONE precise question for Fable in "needs_fable" — a decision or a
  correctness/faithfulness/gap-to-top judgment. Do NOT ask Fable to read or search the codebase; you
  already did that. Give Fable the diff summary + the measurement, not a reading assignment.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# AGENTIC OUTLINE REDESIGN — rich toolkit + hard battery + parallel wheel
2	Author: Fable 5 (brain), 2026-07-11. Supersedes the toolkit + test sections of
3	`docs/fsr_build_plan.md` "AGENTIC OUTLINER LOOP"; the loop driver, gap ledger, faithfulness
4	rules, model lock (glm-5.2 agent / deepseek-v4-pro code), and W0/W1 stay as built.
5	Operator verdict on the current shape: TOO WEAK — 8 tools is a toy, tests are easy/shallow/few,
6	and there is no parallel fast-fix wheel. This document fixes all three.
7	
8	Binding invariants carried forward (unchanged):
9	- Faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI + numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate.
10	  No tool result renders directly; external content re-enters through the fold-in seam
11	  (`_offset_renumber` outline_agent.py:610 + `_stamp_and_delete` :792), computed numbers render
12	  ONLY through the verified lane (tradeoff_modeler ModelSpec), exploratory python stays barred
13	  from rendering.
14	- WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE, never filter/cap (§-1.3); junk-deletion carve-out §-1.3.1 only.
15	- LAW VI: every knob env-tunable. §8.4 resource discipline. Operator is blind: all harness
16	  output is plain text files, never a dashboard-only view.
17	
18	---
19	
20	## PART 1 — THE RICH TOOLKIT
21	
22	Target: ~34 first-party tools across 8 categories, plus an MCP adapter layer that grows the
23	registry to hundreds with ZERO driver changes. Everything speaks the existing contract:
24	`ToolDefinition` / `ToolResult(source_evidence_ids=...)` (tools/tool_registry.py) — the driver
25	(`OutlineAgent`, outline/outline_agent.py:990) does not change shape when tools are added.
26	
27	Legend: [E] = exists today (11 total). [W] = existing dormant module, wrap as a tool (~15-60
28	LOC each). [N] = genuinely new code.
29	
30	### Category A — Retrieval & re-retrieval (get more real evidence)
31	1.  search_more_evidence [E] — full pipe: query-derive -> run_live_retrieval -> S2 stamp -> fold-in (outline_agent.py:638).
32	2.  fetch_url [N] — targeted re-fetch of ONE named URL (agent saw a citation/link inside evidence
33	    and wants the primary). Reuses the run_live_retrieval fetch stack in single-URL mode
34	    (Zyte/crawl4ai fallback chain), then the SAME `_offset_renumber` + `_stamp_and_delete`
35	    fold-in seam as search_more_evidence. This is the tool that turns "source B cites source A"
36	    into actually reading source A.
37	3.  search_scholar [W] — scholarly search via existing tools/openalex_client.py + SemanticScholar
38	    backend already inside live_retriever; returns candidate works (title/venue/year/DOI/abstract)
39	    WITHOUT folding in; agent then fetch_url's the ones it wants. Cheap look-before-fetch.
40	4.  search_corpus [N] — BM25/keyword search over the CURRENT ev_store (rank/score + snippet per
41	    hit). Read-only, no network. The "do we already have this?" tool — mandatory first stop
42	    before any live fetch (the decide prompt says so; the battery asserts it on the saturated
43	    negative-control case).
44	5.  get_evidence [N] — return the FULL text + metadata of one evidence row by ev_id (the agent
45	    currently only sees the digest). Read-only. ~15 LOC.
46	6.  inspect_basket [E] — basket members, corroboration, assignment status (outline_agent.py:863).
47	7.  list_baskets [N] — one-line-per-basket index (id, claim gist, member count, weight range,
48	    assigned section) so the agent can scan the whole corpus shape without 300 inspect calls.
49	
50	### Category B — Document / structure parsing (read what a page really contains)
51	8.  parse_table [W] — extract tables from an evidence row (HTML `<table>` parse + PDF tables via
52	    existing tools/pdf_table_extractor.py) into typed rows {value, unit, row/col label,
53	    ev_id, char-span}. Output cells carry spans so they are verified-compute-eligible.
54	9.  read_spreadsheet [N] — open a fetched .xlsx/.csv artifact (openpyxl/csv), expose
55	    sheet/range/filter reads as typed rows with file+cell provenance. GAIA-class capability.
56	10. parse_figure [N] — chart/figure data extraction from an image or PDF figure via a
57	    vision-capable open-weight model (qwen-VL family per sovereignty; env-locked model id).
58	    HONESTY CONTRACT: output values are tagged `figure_estimated=True` and are NEVER
59	    verified-compute-eligible (no numeric-verbatim span exists); they may guide outline
60	    structure and trigger re-retrieval for the underlying data table, and render only as
61	    labeled approximations. Fail-open to "figure unreadable, disclosed".
62	11. extract_numeric_data [E] — regex/heuristic numeric extraction (tool_registry.py).
63	12. extract_entities [N] — typed entity extraction over selected rows (dates, orgs, tickers,
64	    drugs/dosages, genes, standards, statutes) via the mirror model, each entity pinned to
65	    ev_id+span. Feeds kg_ops and find_contradictions.
66	13. parse_pdf_layout [W] — mineru-based full-layout PDF parse (already deployed for fetch);
67	    exposed as an on-demand tool for a SPECIFIC document the agent cares about (sections,
68	    headings, references list).
69	
70	### Category C — Compute & statistics (derive numbers — two lanes, never confused)
71	14. execute_python [E] — LLM-written sandboxed script (code_executor). EXPLORATORY lane:
72	    agent-internal, BARRED from rendering. Unchanged.
73	15. calculator [N] — single whitelisted-AST arithmetic expression (reuses tradeoff_modeler's
74	    `_eval_formula` machinery, :419). Deterministic, no LLM, no sandbox spin-up, sub-second.
75	    For the 80% of compute needs that are one subtraction/ratio.
76	16. verified_compute [W] — THE moat tool. Wraps synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py
77	    `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) + code_executor execution +
78	    Regime-C replay: agent supplies {question, candidate sourced inputs (ev_id + raw_literal),
79	    formula intent}; the tool validates fail-closed (literal_span_is_faithful :68, AST
80	    whitelist, material dependency) and returns a ComputedClaim that is the ONLY compute
81	    output allowed to render. Currently wired only into old graph_v3 — this registration is
82	    the W3 wire.
83	17. statistical_summary [E] · 18. comparison_table [E] · 19. meta_analysis [E] ·
84	    20. rank_by_impact [E] — unchanged (tool_registry.py).
85	21. time_series_math [N] — deterministic ops over DATED datapoints: delta, %change, CAGR,
86	    moving average, linear trend, YoY alignment. Pure functions over parse_table /
87	    extract_numeric_data outputs; results carry input ev_ids and are verified-compute-eligible
88	    when every input cell has a span (routes through the ModelSpec lane for rendering).
89	22. unit_convert [N] — new tools/unit_converter.py: pint-based unit conversion (mass, volume,
90	    energy, concentration incl. mg/dL<->mmol/L with analyte molar masses table), date
91	    arithmetic (fiscal-year alignment, ISO parsing), currency conversion with a DATED FX table
92	    (ECB reference rates, fetched once + cached as evidence rows so the rate itself is cited).
93	    Deterministic; conversion factor + source disclosed in the result.
94	23. sql_over_tables [N] — load parse_table/read_spreadsheet outputs into the existing
95	    EvidenceDatabase SQLite (tools/evidence_database.py) as ad-hoc tables and query them
96	    (joins, group-bys, conditional sums). Extends query_evidence_sql [E, 24] which stays for
97	    the evidence-metadata table.
98	
99	### Category D — Cross-source reasoning (agreement, conflict, identity)
100	25. agreement_analysis [E] — pairwise Jaccard consensus (tool_registry.py). Kept as the cheap
101	    screen only.
102	26. find_contradictions [N] — the real one: for a claim/aspect, pull the basket's members,
103	    run pairwise NLI-entailment (mirror model, same engine family the verifier uses) +
104	    numeric_comparator on extracted values; returns {agree, conflict, direction-of-conflict}
105	    pairs with ev_ids. Conflicts become outline material ("conflicting evidence" sub-theme),
106	    NEVER silently averaged.
107	27. corroboration_profile [N] — per-basket: distinct works, tier/weight spread,
108	    independent-vs-derivative sources (same-DOI / same-publisher collapse). The "is this
109	    claim really multi-source?" tool.
110	28. citation_lookup [W] — DOI/Crossref/OpenAlex metadata for a work: venue, year, citation
111	    count, OA status, and RETRACTION status (Crossref retraction watch field). Wraps
112	    openalex_client. Retracted -> weight-demote + disclose (never delete: §-1.3, it is
113	    on-topic credible-history content; the retraction itself is evidence).
114	29. kg_ops [N] — lightweight knowledge-graph over extract_entities output held in the
115	    workspace (networkx, in-memory): neighbors(entity), paths(a,b), co-mention clusters.
116	    Structure discovery for outline sub-themes ("these 12 baskets share entity X").
117	    Phase-2 of the build (not needed for battery v1).
118	
119	### Category E — Outline operations (the agent's own artifact)
120	30. update_outline [E] — validated revision ops via outline_revise (outline_agent.py:902).
121	31. coverage_audit [N] — deterministic accounting (never-LLM): unassigned basket ids,
122	    per-section basket counts, residual fraction, sections below floor — the O2/O3 math as an
123	    on-demand tool so the agent can SEE its own coverage instead of waiting for the checklist.
124	    Wraps the section_basket_map stats code.
125	32. preview_section_evidence [N] — for one section: its assigned baskets' claim gists +
126	    weights, exactly what compose will receive. The "walk in the reader's shoes" tool.
127	33. finish_outline [E] — terminal action (driver bounces via checklist; not self-certifying).
128	
129	### Category F — MCP extensibility layer (how 34 becomes hundreds without a rewrite)
130	34. tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py [N] — `McpToolBridge`:
131	    - Config: config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml — list of servers {name, transport
132	      (stdio|http), command/url, allow-list of tool names, content_policy}. LAW VI: file +
133	      env override, no hardcoding. Seat: PG_OUTLINE_MCP (default OFF).
134	    - On startup: open sessions (official `mcp` python SDK), `tools/list` each server,
135	      register every allowed tool as `ToolDefinition(name=f"mcp__{server}__{tool}",
136	      description=<server-provided>, execute=<bridge call>)`. The driver needs ZERO changes —
137	      it already iterates the registry.
138	    - CONTENT POLICY (the faithfulness seam, non-negotiable): every MCP result is classified
139	      by the server's declared `content_policy`:
140	      (a) `external_content` (default) — result text becomes candidate evidence rows and MUST
141	          go through the standard fold-in seam (offset-renumber + URL-dedup + S2 stamp
142	          chrome-delete/topic-judge) before the agent can cite it. It NEVER goes straight
143	          into outline text or ToolResult.markdown as fact.
144	      (b) `pure_compute` — result is a derived value over already-folded evidence; it stays in
145	          the exploratory lane (barred from rendering) unless re-derived through
146	          verified_compute.
147	      This one rule is what lets the toolkit grow to hundreds of tools while the faithfulness
148	      engine remains the only hard gate.
149	    - §8.4: MCP server subprocesses are tracked in state/active_processes.json and torn down
150	      at agent exit.
151	
152	### Scaling the decide step past 20 tools
153	The current `_decide` prompt (outline_agent.py:1152) lists every tool. That holds to ~60 tools
154	(one-liners, ~2k tokens, trivial at glm-5.2's 1M ctx). Changes now:
155	- Registry gains `tags: list[str]` on ToolDefinition and `core: bool`. Decide prompt = CORE
156	  set in full (search_more_evidence, fetch_url, search_corpus, get_evidence, inspect_basket,
157	  update_outline, coverage_audit, calculator, verified_compute, finish_outline) + a grouped
158	  one-line INDEX of everything else by category.
159	- Add meta-tool `list_tools(category|keyword)` returning full descriptions on demand (mirrors
160	  how Claude Code defers 360+ tools behind ToolSearch). When MCP pushes the count past ~60,
161	  the index collapses to category headers + list_tools becomes the discovery path. No driver
162	  rewrite at any size.
163	
164	### New-vs-existing tally
165	Existing kept: 11. Wraps of dormant modules: 6 (search_scholar, parse_table, parse_pdf_layout,
166	verified_compute, citation_lookup, + query_evidence_sql extension). New first-party: 17.
167	Total first-party ≈ 34. MCP layer: unbounded.
168	
169	---
170	
171	## PART 2 — THE HARD EDGE-CASE BATTERY (22 cases)
172	
173	Design rules: every case is (i) REAL data (fixtures = really-fetched rows stored under
174	tests/battery/fixtures/ — LAW II compliant; live cases fetch real primary sources), (ii)
175	answer-verifiable (published gold number) OR behavior-verifiable (a deterministic assertion on
176	the transcript/ledger, never on prose wording), (iii) tagged with the capability it stresses.
177	Benchmark provenance: FinanceBench arXiv:2311.11944, GAIA arXiv:2311.12983, DiscoveryBench
178	arXiv:2407.01725, HLE-style synthesis, FS-Researcher checklist eval (arXiv:2602.01566),
179	DeepTRACE-style adversarial probes.
180	
181	FINANCE
182	- H01 [FinanceBench, gold] Adobe operating income change FY2015->FY2016. Stresses: targeted
183	  fetch of 2 real 10-Ks + parse_table + verified_compute subtract. Expected: gold delta,
184	  trace shows fetch->extract(span-pinned)->compute; a hand-waved number with no compute step
185	  = FAIL even if numerically right.
186	- H02 [FinanceBench, gold] Activision 3-yr (FY2017-19) average capex/revenue. Stresses:
187	  multi-document multi-hop (6 extractions, 3 ratios, 1 mean) inside one verified ModelSpec.
188	  Expected: gold ratio; all 6 inputs literal_span_is_faithful.
189	- H03 [new, gold-derivable] Total shareholder return across a stock split (e.g. NVDA 2021
190	  4-for-1): raw price series naive delta vs split-adjusted. Stresses: domain-trap awareness +
191	  time_series_math. Expected: split detected (it is in the fetched filings/press), adjusted
192	  return computed, adjustment disclosed. Naive unadjusted number rendered as the answer = FAIL.
193	- H04 [new, gold-derivable] Compare FY revenue of a USD-reporting vs EUR-reporting company.
194	  Stresses: unit_convert with DATED FX + fiscal-year alignment (different FY ends). Expected:
195	  conversion at a disclosed dated rate, FY misalignment disclosed; mixing currencies raw = FAIL.
196	- H05 [GAIA L1 adaptation, gold 89706.00] Conditional SUM of food (not drink) sales from a
197	  real xlsx. Stresses: read_spreadsheet + sql_over_tables filtered aggregate. Expected: exact
198	  gold; trace shows the filter predicate.
199	
200	SCIENCE / MEDICAL
201	- H06 [operator's case, gold via SEER] US female breast-cancer age-adjusted incidence change
202	  2000->2019, absolute + percent. Stresses: authoritative-source retrieval (SEER/CDC WONDER)
203	  + verified_compute. Expected: matches re-run of the SEER query; cites SEER not a news

278	(H05,H11,H16,H17), adversarial (H21,H22), domains: finance 5, medical/science 6, math/stats 4,
279	engineering 2, cross-domain behavior 5.
280	
281	Scoring per case = STRUCTURAL assertions only (§-1.1.1 class A): gold-number match within
282	declared tolerance, trace-step presence (fetch->extract->compute), ledger states, retrieval
283	counts, disclosure presence. NO word counts, NO pattern-presence-as-quality. The wheel's
284	mechanical PASS/FAIL is a routing signal for the fix loop; the LOCK verdict additionally
285	requires the class-B fresh-judge line-read (Part 3).
286	
287	---
288	
289	## PART 3 — THE PARALLEL EDGE-CASE HARNESS (the wheel)
290	
291	Purpose: run the whole battery AT ONCE, get a ranked failure list in minutes, fix, re-run only
292	failures, converge fast — then lock with a full run + holdout + fresh-judge read.
293	
294	### Layout
295	- tests/battery/cases/h01_adobe_opinc.yaml ... h22_poison_outlier.yaml — one spec per case:
296	  {id, domain, question, corpus: {fixture: <path> | live: <recipe>}, env_overrides,
297	  budgets {max_turns: 12, wall: 600}, assertions: [{type: gold_number, value, tol, where},
298	  {type: trace_requires, steps: [...]}, {type: retrieval_count, op, n},
299	  {type: ledger_state, ...}, {type: disclosure_required, key}], holdout: false, variants: k}
300	- tests/battery/fixtures/<case>/ — REAL fetched rows (cp2-format JSON), stored once.
301	- scripts/outline_battery.py — the runner (new, ~400 LOC).
302	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/<case_id>/ — per-case sandbox: ev_store copy, checkpoints,
303	  transcript.jsonl, result.json, cost.jsonl, log.
304	- outputs/battery/<run_id>/ranked_failures.md + summary.json — the wheel's output.
305	
306	### Concurrency & isolation
307	- ProcessPoolExecutor, max_workers = PG_BATTERY_CONCURRENCY (default 6; the box is an A100
308	  host with ample CPU — LLM calls are network-bound so 6-12 cases in flight is cheap locally).
309	  Each case = ONE subprocess: own env copy (env_overrides applied to the child env dict, the
310	  parent's os.environ is NEVER mutated), own output dir, own EvidenceDatabase (in-memory /
311	  per-dir sqlite), own checkpoint namespace (workspace out_dir passed per case), own log file.
312	  Zero shared writable state between cases.
313	- Live-retrieval cap: a CROSS-PROCESS counting semaphore (fcntl flock slots file
314	  outputs/battery/.retrieval_slots, PG_BATTERY_LIVE_FETCH_SLOTS default 3) wraps
315	  run_live_retrieval entry inside battery mode — respects API rate limits and the container
316	  PID cap (fetch concurrency already 16). Fixture-corpus cases (17 of 22) do zero live fetch
317	  and parallelize freely; only H01/H02/H06/H18 + live variants contend for slots.
318	- OpenRouter: per-case client instances, per-case cost ledger; battery aborts a case (not the
319	  run) at PG_BATTERY_COST_CAP_PER_CASE.
320	- Timeout: per-case hard kill at budget wall + 120s grace (the process is killed, result =
321	  TIMEOUT, severity S3) — one wedged case can never stall the wheel.
322	
323	### Result collection & failure ranking
324	Each case emits result.json: {case_id, variant, assertions: [{name, pass, expected, actual}],
325	final_answer, turns, retrieval_calls, wall_s, cost, transcript_path}. The collector ranks by
326	severity:
327	- S0 FAITHFULNESS BREACH — a wrong/unsupported number rendered as VERIFIED; injection obeyed;
328	  id-collision assert tripped; retracted source used as sole support silently. Blocks
329	  everything; fix first.
330	- S1 WRONG ANSWER, UNDISCLOSED — gold missed and no UNFILLED/uncertainty disclosure.
331	- S2 CAPABILITY MISS, HONEST — gold missed but correctly disclosed as unfilled/approximate
332	  (the agent knew its limits). These are the capability roadmap, not blockers.
333	- S3 BEHAVIOR — redundant retrieval on saturated, budget/wall overshoot, degenerate looping,
334	  timeout.
335	- S4 cosmetic.
336	ranked_failures.md lists S0 first, each with: the failed assertion diff, the exact transcript
337	line range, and the suspected component (tool name from the trace). Ties break by frequency
338	across paraphrase variants (a case failing on all 3 variants outranks a 1-of-3 flake).
339	
340	### The fast test->fix->retest loop (wheel cadence)
341	1. `python scripts/outline_battery.py --all` — full battery, 6-way. Expected wall: <30 min
342	   (each case ≤600s, mostly parallel).
343	2. Read ranked_failures.md top-down. Fix the top S0/S1 cluster (Fable diagnoses root cause,
344	   Opus builds, per the standing workflow).
345	3. `--only-failed --baseline <run_id>` — re-runs ONLY previously-failing cases (+their
346	   variants). Minutes, not hours.
347	4. Repeat 2-3 until zero S0/S1 on the non-holdout set.
348	5. LOCK GATE: full `--all --include-holdout` clean of S0/S1 + a FRESH judge (never the
349	   builder — §-1.1, independent line-audit rule) reads 5 sampled transcripts line-by-line at
350	   the context level and confirms the PASSes are real. Only then is the wheel iteration
351	   signed off.
352	Every run appends one line to outputs/battery/history.jsonl (run_id, git sha, pass counts by
353	severity) — the convergence record the operator can hear read out plainly.
354	
355	### Anti-overfit (how it stays general)
356	- HOLDOUT: ~25% of cases (H04, H13, H15, H17, H22 initially) are excluded from all fix
357	  iterations; run ONLY at lock gates. A fix that passes iteration cases but fails holdout is
358	  overfit by definition — root-cause again.
359	- PARAPHRASE VARIANTS: each behavioral case carries k=3 auto-generated question rewrites
360	  (generated once, frozen in the yaml); assertions are variant-invariant. Kills
361	  prompt-string-matching fixes.
362	- ASSERTIONS ON BEHAVIOR AND GOLD ONLY — never on tool-call ORDER, never on prose wording,
363	  never on word/citation counts (§-1.1). Multiple valid tool paths to the same gold all pass.
364	- NO CASE-ID LEAKAGE: CI grep gate — battery case ids and gold literals must not appear
365	  anywhere under src/ (a fix referencing h01/89706.00 in production code fails the gate).
366	- DOMAIN BALANCE: the ranked report also groups failures by domain; a fix wave is not
367	  accepted if it improves one domain while regressing another (history.jsonl makes this
368	  visible).
369	- Rotation: each lock adds 2-3 NEW cases sourced from fresh benchmark items before the next
370	  wheel iteration, so the battery is a moving target.
371	
372	---
373	
374	## PART 4 — EXACT BUILD STEPS (file:line, in order)
375	
376	T1 — TOOLKIT CORE (biggest capability jump first)
377	1. NEW src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_toolkit.py — `register_outline_toolkit(registry,
378	   workspace, agent_model, deadline)`: implements/wires fetch_url, search_corpus,
379	   get_evidence, list_baskets, calculator, coverage_audit, preview_section_evidence,
380	   find_contradictions, corroboration_profile. fetch_url MUST reuse `_offset_renumber`
381	   (outline/outline_agent.py:610) + `_stamp_and_delete` (:792) — move those two into
382	   outline_toolkit.py (or a shared outline/_fold_in.py) and import back into outline_agent.py
383	   so both tools share the ONE fold-in seam.
384	2. EDIT outline/outline_agent.py:1030 `_build_registry` — after the three existing
385	   registrations, call `register_outline_toolkit(...)`. Add `tags`/`core` fields to
386	   ToolDefinition (tools/tool_registry.py:44 dataclass) with defaults so all existing
387	   call sites are untouched.
388	3. EDIT outline/outline_agent.py:1152 `_decide` — CORE-set-in-full + categorized one-line
389	   index prompt shape; add `list_tools` meta-tool.
390	4. NEW tools/unit_converter.py (pint + molar-mass table + dated ECB FX cache-as-evidence) and
391	   register unit_convert + time_series_math (pure functions module
392	   tools/time_series_math.py).
393	5. WIRE verified_compute: NEW wrapper in outline_toolkit.py around
394	   synthesis/tradeoff_modeler.py `build_quantified_spec` (:782) + `render_script` (:1196) +
395	   tools/code_executor.execute_analysis_script; ComputedClaim lands in the workspace flagged
396	   verified-lane; exploratory execute_python results get `render_barred=True` in
397	   ToolResult.statistics (compose already never reads exploratory results — assert it).
398	6. Tests: tests/polaris_graph/test_outline_toolkit.py — per-tool unit tests incl. fold-in
399	   id-collision assert, unit_convert round-trips, calculator AST rejection cases.
400	
401	T2 — BATTERY HARNESS + FIRST 12 CASES
402	7. NEW scripts/outline_battery.py (runner: case loader, ProcessPool, child-env isolation,
403	   flock retrieval slots, per-case kill, collector, ranked_failures.md writer,
404	   history.jsonl).
405	8. NEW tests/battery/cases/*.yaml — H01, H05, H07, H08, H09, H10, H11, H14, H18, H19, H20,
406	   H21 first (they exercise T1 tools + carry the W1 acceptance forward). Fixtures: fetch the
407	   real corpora ONCE on the box (fixture-builder mode `--record` in the runner), commit under
408	   tests/battery/fixtures/.
409	9. Run the wheel; fix to zero S0/S1 on these 12.
410	
411	T3 — PARSE + SPREADSHEET TOOLS + REMAINING CASES
412	10. Register parse_table (wrap tools/pdf_table_extractor.py + html table parse),
413	    read_spreadsheet (openpyxl), sql_over_tables (extend tools/evidence_database.py with
414	    ad-hoc table load), extract_entities, search_scholar + citation_lookup (wrap
415	    tools/openalex_client.py + Crossref retraction field), parse_pdf_layout (mineru hook).
416	11. Add cases H02, H03, H06, H12, H16 + holdout set H04, H13, H15, H17, H22. Wheel to zero
417	    S0/S1 non-holdout, then first LOCK GATE (holdout + fresh-judge read).
418	
419	T4 — MCP LAYER + FIGURE READ
420	12. NEW tools/mcp_tool_adapter.py + config/settings/outline_mcp_servers.yaml (seat
421	    PG_OUTLINE_MCP=0 default OFF; content_policy enforcement at the bridge; §8.4 teardown).
422	    First servers: a filesystem server (report artifacts) and a fetch server (redundant
423	    with fetch_url — used as the adapter's own integration test).
424	13. parse_figure (qwen-VL via OpenRouter, figure_estimated contract) + kg_ops (networkx over
425	    extract_entities). Add H17 to the active set.
426	14. Final wheel to lock: full battery + holdout clean + fresh-judge line-read + the standing
427	    W-series acceptance (loop fires on thin, silent on saturated) re-confirmed.
428	
429	Throughout: PG_OUTLINE_AGENT stays the master seat (default OFF, byte-identical legacy);
430	every new tool honors the shared wall (`retrieval_deadline_monotonic`, outline_agent.py:1010)
431	and the gap ledger contract; docs/fsr_build_plan.md gets a pointer line to this file.
432	
433	---
434	
435	## PART 5 — WHY THIS STAYS GENERAL (not overfit)
436	
437	1. Tools are domain-free primitives (fetch, parse, convert, compute, compare); zero domain
438	   templates. Domain knowledge enters only through evidence content and weights (§-1.3).
439	2. The battery spans 6 domains and asserts BEHAVIOR + published gold, never prose or tool
440	   order; paraphrase variants + holdout + case rotation + the no-case-id-leakage grep gate
441	   make memorized fixes structurally impossible to land.
442	3. The faithfulness engine is untouched and remains the only hard gate; every new content
443	   path (fetch_url, MCP, figures) is forced through the SAME fold-in seam and the SAME
444	   verified-compute lane, so capability growth can never widen the rendering surface.
445	4. Mechanical battery PASS/FAIL is a routing signal for the fix loop only (§-1.1.1 class A);
446	   every lock requires the independent fresh-judge context-level line-read (class B). A
447	   toolkit that games the assertions still cannot pass a human-grade read of its transcripts.

566	def detect_sourced_conflicts(
567	    sourced_numbers: list[dict[str, Any]],
568	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
569	    """Flag datapoints that describe the SAME quantity (same label+unit) but whose
570	    values disagree by > ``PG_CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL``. Telemetry only — does not
571	    gate. Returns a list of conflict records {label, unit, values, rel_spread}."""
572	    groups: dict[tuple[str, str], list[float]] = {}
573	    for dp in sourced_numbers:
574	        label = str(dp.get("label", "")).strip().lower()
575	        unit = str(dp.get("unit", "")).strip().lower()
576	        if not label:
577	            continue
578	        try:
579	            val = float(dp.get("value"))
580	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
581	            continue
582	        groups.setdefault((label, unit), []).append(val)
583	
584	    conflicts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
585	    for (label, unit), vals in groups.items():
586	        if len(vals) < 2:
587	            continue
588	        lo, hi = min(vals), max(vals)
589	        denom = max(abs(lo), abs(hi), 1e-9)
590	        rel_spread = abs(hi - lo) / denom
591	        if rel_spread > _CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL:
592	            conflicts.append({
593	                "label": label, "unit": unit,
594	                "values": sorted(set(vals)), "rel_spread": rel_spread,
595	            })
596	    return conflicts

1885	def verify_modeled_atom(
1886	    sentence: str,
1887	    calc_match: "re.Match[str]",
1888	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any],
1889	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1890	) -> SentenceVerification:
1891	    """Verify a sentence carrying a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token.
1892	
1893	    Fail-closed checks (brief §1.5):
1894	      (a) (model_id, spec_hash) resolves in the RUN-SCOPED registry — a stale or
1895	          foreign model reference fails (P7-19).
1896	      (b) the field exists in that model's executed result.
1897	      (c) the rendered number IMMEDIATELY before the token equals the field's
1898	          canonical ``display_value`` — exact display-string match OR a tight
1899	          numeric backstop (D2). Adjacency means a modeled-assumption number
1900	          elsewhere in the sentence cannot satisfy the token (P7-20).
1901	      (d) every modeled input USED by the field is labeled "(modeled assumption)"
1902	          in the sentence (P7-7/P7-8).
1903	      (e) the field's sourced inputs all resolve in the evidence pool.
1904	
1905	    On PASS, returns SentenceVerification whose ``tokens`` are the source-input
1906	    ProvenanceTokens (so resolve_provenance_to_citations cites the inputs) — the
1907	    calc token is stripped downstream. On any failure → is_verified=False (the
1908	    whole sentence is dropped).
1909	    """
1910	    model_id = calc_match.group("model_id")
1911	    spec_hash = calc_match.group("spec_hash")
1912	    field_id = calc_match.group("field")
1913	    failures: list[str] = []
1914	
1915	    result = quantified_models.get((model_id, spec_hash))
1916	    if result is None:
1917	        return SentenceVerification(
1918	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1919	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_model_not_in_registry:{model_id}:{spec_hash}"],
1920	        )
1921	    fld = getattr(result, "fields", {}).get(field_id)
1922	    if not isinstance(fld, dict):
1923	        return SentenceVerification(
1924	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1925	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_field_unknown:{field_id}"],
1926	        )
1927	
1928	    display_value = str(fld.get("display_value", ""))
1929	
1930	    # (c) adjacency + equality (Codex diff-gate P1-1/P1-2). The rendered number
1931	    # IMMEDIATELY before the token must canonicalize to EXACTLY the field's
1932	    # display_value. Two fail-closed properties:
1933	    #   - FULL-number compare (not `before.endswith(display_value)`, which would
1934	    #     accept "123.40%" for a "23.40%" field — the wrong number ends with the
1935	    #     canonical string).
1936	    #   - RE-CANONICALIZE the parsed adjacent value through the SAME pinned
1937	    #     formatter and require an exact string match (no magnitude-scaled rel-tol
1938	    #     drift, which would accept "$1,000,000,000,999.00" for a
1939	    #     "$1,000,000,000,000.00" field). A benign reformat (missing $/commas) that
1940	    #     canonicalizes to the same string still passes; any real numeric
1941	    #     difference fails.
1942	    before = sentence[: calc_match.start()]
1943	    adj = _CALC_ADJACENT_NUMBER_RE.search(before)
1944	    if adj is None:
1945	        failures.append("calc_no_adjacent_number")
1946	    else:
1947	        adj_str = adj.group(1).strip()
1948	        ok = adj_str == display_value
1949	        if not ok:
1950	            adj_val = _calc_parse_number(adj_str)
1951	            if adj_val is not None:
1952	                from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (
1953	                    _canonical_display,
1954	                )
1955	                recanon = _canonical_display(
1956	                    adj_val,
1957	                    str(fld.get("unit", "")),
1958	                    str(fld.get("display_kind", "number")),
1959	                )
1960	                ok = recanon == display_value
1961	        if not ok:
1962	            failures.append(f"calc_number_mismatch:{adj_str}!={display_value}")
1963	
1964	    # (d) modeled-assumption labeling
1965	    modeled_used = fld.get("modeled_used") or []
1966	    if modeled_used and _MODELED_ASSUMPTION_LABEL not in sentence:
1967	        failures.append(
1968	            "calc_modeled_assumption_unlabeled:" + ",".join(map(str, modeled_used))
1969	        )
1970	
1971	    # (e) sourced-input evidence resolution + ProvenanceToken construction
1972	    src_tokens: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
1973	    for t in (fld.get("sourced_tokens") or []):
1974	        ev_id = str(t.get("ev_id", ""))
1975	        if ev_id not in evidence_pool:
1976	            failures.append(f"calc_input_ev_not_in_pool:{ev_id}")
1977	            continue
1978	        src_tokens.append(ProvenanceToken(
1979	            evidence_id=ev_id,
1980	            start=int(t.get("start", 0)),
1981	            end=int(t.get("end", 0)),
1982	            raw=str(t.get("raw", "")),
1983	        ))
1984	
1985	    if failures:
1986	        return SentenceVerification(
1987	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1988	            failure_reasons=failures,
1989	        )
1990	    return SentenceVerification(
1991	        sentence=sentence, tokens=src_tokens, is_verified=True,
1992	        failure_reasons=[], soft_warnings=["regime_c_modeled_verified"],
1993	    )

1999	# ROOT (grounded in drb_72_ai_labor/verification_details.json): the entailment judge
2000	# NEUTRAL-dropped a genuinely-grounded finding —
2001	#   sentence: "Eloundou and colleagues propose a framework ... [#ev:eloundou_gpts_are_gpts:0-800]."
2002	#   reason:   "entailment_failed:...verdict=NEUTRAL:reason=The sentence adds the
2003	#              unsupported specificity of the authors' names (Eloundou and ...)"
2004	# The cited evidence row carries authors=['Eloundou T','Manning S','Mishkin P','Rock D']
2005	# as REAL bibliographic metadata, but the fetched `direct_quote` span omits the byline,
2006	# so the judge — which only sees the cited SPAN text — treats the author attribution as
2007	# unsupported specificity and the sentence drops.
2008	#
2009	# FIX: when the SENTENCE names an author who is genuinely in a cited source's `authors`
2010	# metadata, append that source's real author list to the SPAN TEXT PASSED TO THE
2011	# ENTAILMENT JUDGE ONLY, so a true attribution is groundable. §-1.3-safe / faithfulness-
2012	# TIGHTENING-not-relaxing: it binds ONLY REAL metadata from the evidence record (never a
2013	# claim, never a number, never a topic), and ONLY when the sentence actually names one of
2014	# the real authors — a fabricated attribution ("Smith found ...", no such author) never
2015	# matches, so it still NEUTRAL-drops. The mechanical numeric/content-overlap gates run
2016	# EARLIER against the span alone and are unaffected. LAW VI kill-switch:
2017	# PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND=0 reverts byte-identical (no annotation appended).
2018	_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND_ENV = "PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND"
2019	# A surname must be at least this long to be matched as a whole word in the sentence
2020	# (guards against a 2-3 char initial/common-word false match, e.g. "Rock D" -> "Rock").
2021	_AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN = 4
2022	_AUTHOR_NAME_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'’\-]+")
2023	_AUTHOR_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"\s*(?:;|,| and | & )\s*")
2024

2026	def _author_metadata_bind_enabled() -> bool:
2027	    """PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND gate (default ON)."""
2028	    return os.getenv(_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
2029	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
2030	    )

2033	def _author_entries(authors: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
2034	    """(surname, full_entry) pairs from an evidence row's ``authors`` metadata.
2035	
2036	    Accepts a list of author strings or a single delimited string. The surname is the
2037	    longest alphabetic token >= _AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN in each entry ("Eloundou T" ->
2038	    "Eloundou"; "J. Satyadhar" -> "Satyadhar"). Entries with no long-enough token are
2039	    skipped (cannot be matched safely)."""
2040	    if not authors:
2041	        return []
2042	    if isinstance(authors, str):
2043	        entries = [a for a in _AUTHOR_SPLIT_RE.split(authors) if a and a.strip()]
2044	    elif isinstance(authors, (list, tuple)):
2045	        entries = [str(a) for a in authors if str(a).strip()]
2046	    else:
2047	        return []
2048	    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
2049	    for entry in entries:
2050	        entry = entry.strip()
2051	        toks = [t for t in _AUTHOR_NAME_TOKEN_RE.findall(entry) if len(t) >= _AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN]
2052	        if not toks:
2053	            continue
2054	        surname = max(toks, key=len)
2055	        out.append((surname, entry))
2056	    return out

2059	def _author_metadata_annotation(
2060	    sentence: str,
2061	    tokens: list["ProvenanceToken"],
2062	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
2063	) -> str:
2064	    """Bibliographic-metadata line binding the REAL authors of a cited source when the
2065	    SENTENCE names one of them, else "" (byte-identical / no augmentation).
2066	
2067	    Fires per cited source: if any of that source's author surnames appears as a whole
2068	    word in ``sentence``, the source's FULL author list is appended (so "Eloundou and
2069	    colleagues" is grounded by both Eloundou and the co-authors). Returns "" when the
2070	    leg is disabled, the sentence names no real author, or no cited source carries
2071	    author metadata."""
2072	    if not _author_metadata_bind_enabled() or not sentence or not tokens:
2073	        return ""
2074	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
2075	    lists: list[str] = []
2076	    for tok in tokens:
2077	        ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
2078	        if ev is None or tok.evidence_id in seen_ids:
2079	            continue
2080	        seen_ids.add(tok.evidence_id)
2081	        pairs = _author_entries(ev.get("authors") if isinstance(ev, dict) else None)
2082	        if not pairs:
2083	            continue
2084	        named = any(
2085	            re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(surname)}\b", sentence) for surname, _ in pairs
2086	        )
2087	        if named:
2088	            full_list = ", ".join(full for _, full in pairs)
2089	            if full_list:
2090	                lists.append(full_list)
2091	    if not lists:
2092	        return ""
2093	    # dedupe preserving order (two cited spans of the SAME paper -> one metadata line)
2094	    uniq = list(dict.fromkeys(lists))
2095	    return " Cited source authors: " + "; ".join(uniq) + "."

2098	def verify_sentence_provenance(
2099	    sentence: str,
2100	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
2101	    *,
2102	    require_number_match: bool = True,
2103	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
2104	    allow_local_window_fallback: bool = True,
2105	) -> SentenceVerification:
2106	    """Verify every provenance token in a sentence.
2107	
2108	    Checks:
2109	      1. Evidence ID exists in pool.
2110	      2. Span bounds are within evidence direct_quote length.
2111	      3. If require_number_match AND the sentence contains numbers,
2112	         each number must appear in the claimed span text.
2113	
2114	    Phase 7 (#991) — Regime C router: when ``quantified_models`` is supplied
2115	    (run-scoped registry keyed by (model_id, spec_hash)) AND the sentence carries
2116	    a ``[#calc:...]`` token, the sentence is FORCE-ROUTED to
2117	    ``verify_modeled_atom`` BEFORE the Regime-A machinery below and that result is
2118	    returned. ``quantified_models=None`` (default) skips the router entirely →
2119	    Regime A is byte-identical.
2120	
2121	    I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) — ``allow_local_window_fallback`` (default
2122	    True, byte-identical to pre-iter-2). When FALSE, the two gap-#18 LOCAL-WINDOW
2123	    rescue sites are DISABLED so the sentence passes ONLY if its OWN bound span
2124	    directly clears the content/numeric floor AND directly entails — no different
2125	    in-row window may rescue it. This is set FALSE from ``_try_reanchor`` so the
2126	    re-anchored token's FINAL BOUND SPAN ITSELF DIRECTLY SUPPORTS the claim; the
2127	    Codex iter-1 P1 leak (a candidate span kept on the back of a DIFFERENT
2128	    entailing window while the token stays bound to the non-entailing span) is
2129	    structurally closed. Production strict_verify keeps the default True, so the

741	    """Write the audit/replay bundle (gap-19): spec + rendered script + per-field
742	    canonical display values + spec_hash."""

751	             "raw_literal": s.raw_literal, "literal_span": [s.literal_start, s.literal_end]}

766	                "value": f["value"], "display_value": f["display_value"],

782	def bind_calc_tokens(prose: str, result: QuantifiedResult) -> str:
783	    """Replace each ``{{calc:<field>}}`` placeholder with the field's canonical
784	    display value IMMEDIATELY followed by its calc token, so the verifier binds
785	    the token to the exact rendered number (token adjacency, brief §1.4)."""
786	    def _sub(m: re.Match) -> str:
787	        fid = m.group("field")
788	        disp = result.display_value(fid)
789	        if disp is None:
790	            return m.group(0)  # leave unknown placeholder untouched (will fail verify)
791	        return f"{disp}{result.calc_token(fid)}"
792	
793	    return _CALC_PLACEHOLDER_RE.sub(_sub, prose)

620	def _offset_renumber(
621	    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], offset: int, existing_ids: set[str],
622	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
623	    """THE sharp seam (design §): renumber ``new_rows`` to ``ev_{offset+i}`` and HARD
624	    fail-loud assert the new id set never intersects ``existing_ids``. A collision here is a
625	    programming bug in the offset computation, not a data condition — it must never be
626	    swallowed (LAW II: fail loudly, never silently degrade)."""
627	    renumbered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
628	    new_ids: set[str] = set()
629	    for i, row in enumerate(new_rows):
630	        new_id = f"ev_{offset + i:03d}"
631	        if new_id in existing_ids or new_id in new_ids:
632	            raise AssertionError(
633	                f"outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED: {new_id!r} already present "
634	                f"(existing_ids has it: {new_id in existing_ids}; batch dup: {new_id in new_ids}). "
635	                "This must never happen — the offset computation has a bug."
636	            )
637	        new_ids.add(new_id)
638	        copied = dict(row)
639	        copied["evidence_id"] = new_id
640	        renumbered.append(copied)
641	    assert new_ids.isdisjoint(existing_ids), (
642	        "outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED post-hoc: new ids intersect existing ids "
643	        f"({sorted(new_ids & existing_ids)})"
644	    )
645	    return renumbered

648	async def _tool_search_more_evidence(
649	    workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
650	    agent_model: str,
651	    *,
652	    section: str = "",
653	    aspect: str = "",
654	    domain: Optional[str] = None,
655	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
656	    **_ignored: Any,
657	) -> ToolResult:
658	    """The retrieval pipe as a ReAct tool. Steps (design §): per-todo query derivation
659	    (scope-anchored, ``_is_status_leak`` guard) -> ``run_live_retrieval(anchor_seed=False)`` ->
660	    ``merge_retrieval_results`` -> offset-renumber (hard assert) + URL-dedup vs existing ->
661	    S2 stamp pass (chrome delete + topic-judge fail-open) -> fold into ``ev_store`` -> disclosed
662	    ``ToolResult``. One retrieval in flight at a time (§8.4 — this coroutine is awaited, never
663	    fired concurrently by the driver)."""
664	    aspect_text = str(aspect or "").strip() or str(section or "").strip()
665	    if not aspect_text:
666	        return ToolResult(
667	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
668	            markdown="search_more_evidence requires a `section` or `aspect`.",
669	            error="missing_aspect",
670	        )
671	
672	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
673	        _is_status_leak,
674	    )
675	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
676	
677	    query_derive_max_tokens = _env_int(
678	        "PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS", PG_OUTLINE_QUERY_DERIVE_MAX_TOKENS_DEFAULT,
679	    )
680	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=agent_model)
681	    try:
682	        derive_prompt = (
683	            "Write ONE web-search query for the SUB-TOPIC below, kept STRICTLY within the "
684	            "scope of the RESEARCH QUESTION (carry its subject, domain and key entities; do "
685	            "NOT broaden the query into the sub-topic's generic field). Query only, one line, "
686	            "no quotes.\n\n"
687	            f"RESEARCH QUESTION:\n{workspace.research_question}\n\nSUB-TOPIC:\n{aspect_text}"
688	        )
689	        try:
690	            # Iter-6 P0 fix: same reasoning-pool bound as the checklist/decide calls above —
691	            # this call already fails open on ANY exception (including a truncation), but an
692	            # unbounded-effort reasoning prelude on a dense corpus still needlessly degrades a
693	            # one-line query derivation to the raw aspect text every time; bounding it means the
694	            # derived (scope-anchored) query actually gets used instead of the coarser fallback.
695	            resp = await client.generate(
696	                prompt=derive_prompt, max_tokens=query_derive_max_tokens, temperature=0.2,
697	                reasoning_max_tokens=_reasoning_max_tokens(),
698	            )
699	            derived = (resp.content or "").strip().splitlines()[0].strip().strip('"') if (
700	                resp.content or ""
701	            ).strip() else ""
702	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — query derivation is a small text call; degrade to raw aspect
703	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] query derivation failed, using raw aspect: %s", exc)
704	            derived = ""
705	    finally:
706	        if hasattr(client, "close"):
707	            try:
708	                await client.close()
709	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
710	                pass
711	
712	    query = derived or aspect_text
713	    if _is_status_leak(query, workspace.research_question):
714	        return ToolResult(
715	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
716	            markdown=f"Derived query was a status-leak, aborted: {query[:120]!r}",
717	            error="status_leak_aborted",
718	        )
719	
720	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # noqa: PLC0415
721	        LiveRetrievalResult, run_live_retrieval,
722	    )
723	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.fs_researcher_query_gen import (  # noqa: PLC0415
724	        merge_retrieval_results,
725	    )
726	
727	    t0 = time.monotonic()
728	    try:
729	        # BUG FOUND IN W1 LIVE ACCEPTANCE RUN (fixed same-iter): ``anchor_seed=False`` makes
730	        # ``run_live_retrieval`` build ``all_queries`` from ``amplified_queries`` ONLY (the
731	        # verbatim ``research_question`` is NOT auto-fired when the anchor is suppressed — see
732	        # the function's own docstring). The gap query MUST be passed via ``amplified_queries``;
733	        # ``research_question`` stays the WORKSPACE question (scope-validator anchor text) so a
734	        # scoped sub-query is validated against the real topic, not against itself.
735	        raw_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
736	            run_live_retrieval,
737	            research_question=workspace.research_question,
738	            amplified_queries=[query],
739	            domain=domain,
740	            anchor_seed=False,
741	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
742	        )
743	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a single retrieval round must not crash the loop
744	        return ToolResult(
745	            success=False, tool_name="search_more_evidence",
746	            markdown=f"run_live_retrieval raised for query {query!r}: {exc}",
747	            error=str(exc)[:500],
748	        )
749	    elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
750	
751	    merged = merge_retrieval_results([raw_result], LiveRetrievalResult)
752	    fetched_rows = list(getattr(merged, "evidence_rows", None) or [])
753	    n_fetched_of = f"{len(fetched_rows)} of {getattr(merged, 'candidates_total', len(fetched_rows))}"
754	
755	    existing_urls = workspace.existing_urls()
756	    deduped_rows = [
757	        r for r in fetched_rows
758	        if str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip() not in existing_urls
759	        or not str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
760	    ]
761	    n_url_dup = len(fetched_rows) - len(deduped_rows)
762	
763	    offset = workspace.next_ev_offset()
764	    existing_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
765	    renumbered = _offset_renumber(deduped_rows, offset, existing_ids)
766	
767	    # S2 stamp pass: chrome delete (content-integrity) + topic-judge fail-open off-topic delete.
768	    kept_rows, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic = await _stamp_and_delete(
769	        renumbered, workspace.research_question, agent_model,
770	    )
771	
772	    for row in kept_rows:
773	        workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row
774	
775	    n_kept = len(kept_rows)
776	    n_deleted = len(deleted_chrome) + len(deleted_offtopic)
777	    disclosure = (
778	        f"search_more_evidence[{aspect_text[:60]!r}] query={query!r} fetched {n_fetched_of}, "
779	        f"url-dup dropped {n_url_dup}, kept {n_kept}, deleted {n_deleted} "
780	        f"(chrome={len(deleted_chrome)}, off-topic={len(deleted_offtopic)}) in {elapsed:.1f}s"
781	    )
782	    workspace.disclose(disclosure)
783	
784	    md_lines = [f"**{disclosure}**", ""]
785	    for row in kept_rows[:10]:
786	        md_lines.append(
787	            f"- {row.get('evidence_id')}: {str(row.get('title') or '')[:80]} "
788	            f"[CITE:{row.get('evidence_id')}]"
789	        )
790	    if len(kept_rows) > 10:
791	        md_lines.append(f"- ... and {len(kept_rows) - 10} more")
792	
793	    return ToolResult(
794	        success=n_kept > 0,
795	        tool_name="search_more_evidence",
796	        markdown="\n".join(md_lines),
797	        source_evidence_ids=[r["evidence_id"] for r in kept_rows],
798	        error=("" if n_kept > 0 else "no_new_evidence_survived_screen"),
799	    )

802	async def _stamp_and_delete(
803	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
804	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
805	    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
806	    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
807	    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
808	    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
809	    if not rows:
810	        return [], [], []
811	
812	    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415
813	
814	    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
815	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
816	    for row in rows:
817	        body = max(
818	            (
819	                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
820	                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
821	                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
822	                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
823	                )
824	            ),
825	            key=len, default="",
826	        )
827	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
828	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
829	        try:
830	            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
831	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
832	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
833	            is_junk, cls = False, ""
834	        if is_junk:
835	            copied = dict(row)
836	            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
837	            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
838	        else:
839	            survivors.append(row)
840	
841	    if not survivors:
842	        return [], deleted_chrome, []
843	
844	    try:
845	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
846	            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
847	        )
848	        if not topic_gate_enabled():
849	            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
850	        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
851	            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
852	        )
853	        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
854	            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
855	        )
856	        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
857	        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
858	        deleted_offtopic = []
859	        for row in dropped:

860	            copied = dict(row)
861	            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
862	            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
863	        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
864	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
865	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
866	        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []

1000	class OutlineAgent:
1001	    """The per-turn decide/execute loop over the outline workspace. Mirrors the SHAPE of
1002	    ``ReactAnalysisAgent._run_react`` (react_agent.py:690) — decide -> execute -> record ->
1003	    check-stop — without inheriting from it (design: WIRING, not building)."""
1004	
1005	    def __init__(
1006	        self,
1007	        workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
1008	        *,
1009	        agent_model: Optional[str] = None,
1010	        max_turns: Optional[int] = None,
1011	        wall_seconds: Optional[int] = None,
1012	        domain: Optional[str] = None,
1013	        retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
1014	    ):
1015	        self.workspace = workspace
1016	        self.agent_model = agent_model or outliner_agent_model()
1017	        self.max_turns = max_turns if max_turns is not None else _max_turns()
1018	        self.wall_seconds = wall_seconds if wall_seconds is not None else _wall_seconds()
1019	        self.domain = domain
1020	        # Iter-2 P1-3 fix: the OUTER while-loop only checked the wall BETWEEN turns, so a
1021	        # single in-flight ``search_more_evidence`` call could itself run well past the wall
1022	        # (observed: 218s overshoot). ``run_live_retrieval`` already has its own internal
1023	        # mid-fetch wall defense (WALL-03, ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic``) but nothing wired
1024	        # it in — the parameter was accepted here and silently stayed ``None`` (unbounded) on
1025	        # every call. Default it from the SAME wall the outer loop uses, anchored at workspace
1026	        # start, so the in-flight fetch/enrich/classify sub-waits inside a single search call
1027	        # are ALSO bounded by the agent's own wall. An explicit override (e.g. a shared
1028	        # pipeline-wide deadline) still wins.
1029	        self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic = (
1030	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic
1031	            if retrieval_deadline_monotonic is not None
1032	            else workspace.started_monotonic + self.wall_seconds
1033	        )
1034	        self.registry: ToolRegistry = self._build_registry()
1035	        if workspace.notebook is None:
1036	            workspace.notebook = AnalysisNotebook(
1037	                query=workspace.research_question, evidence_ids=list(workspace.ev_store.keys()),
1038	            )
1039	
1040	    def _build_registry(self) -> ToolRegistry:
1041	        registry = build_default_registry()  # the 8 analysis tools, byte-identical reuse
1042	
1043	        async def _exec_inspect_basket(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1044	            return await _tool_inspect_basket(self.workspace, **kw)
1045	
1046	        async def _exec_search_more_evidence(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1047	            return await _tool_search_more_evidence(
1048	                self.workspace, self.agent_model, domain=self.domain,
1049	                retrieval_deadline_monotonic=self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic, **kw,
1050	            )
1051	
1052	        async def _exec_update_outline(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1053	            return await _tool_update_outline(self.workspace, **kw)
1054

800	    the exact rejecting gate was invisible post-run — the I-fix-001 silent no-op:
801	    a cert run recorded ``firing_status=spec_validation_rejected`` with NO
802	    attributable gate. The reason channel makes the silent failure name itself.

805	        # One channel for every fail-closed exit: log (stderr) AND surface the
806	        # reason to the caller's telemetry (durable manifest). Returns None so
807	        # callers write ``return _reject(...)``.
808	        logger.warning("[tradeoff_modeler] reject: %s", reason)

1	"""I-meta-005 Phase 7 (#991) smoke — quantified trade-off (gap 9, PAL rewire).
2	
3	SPEND-FREE: ``build_quantified_spec`` takes a FAKE ``spec_llm`` (no LLM); the
4	deterministic ``render_script`` runs the FIXED Python via the existing sandbox
5	(``execute_analysis_script``) with NO network/live client. Regime C verification
6	is pure. Plain assertions, no unittest.mock.
7	
8	P7-1 OFF byte-identity              P7-11 formula AST + dependency reject
9	P7-2 sandbox unchanged              P7-12 calc-token strip (no leak)
10	P7-3 deterministic Execute          P7-13 sourced-conflict telemetry
11	P7-4 Regime C PASS                  P7-14 audit replay (round-trip + stable hash)
12	P7-5 Regime C FAIL (number!=disp)   P7-15 modeled base+sweep+break-even
13	P7-6 sourced literal not in evid.   P7-16 scaled-literal recovery
14	P7-7 modeled unlabeled -> DROP      P7-17 exact-one-match duplicate reject
15	P7-8 modeled labeled -> VERIFIED    P7-18 cancellation/zero-effect reject
16	P7-9 input neither -> None          P7-19 run-scoped stale model reject
17	P7-10 sentence-level multi-number   P7-20 token adjacency
18	"""

60	    return {
61	        "data_type": "cost", "label": label, "context": context,
62	        "value": str(value), "unit": unit, "year": "2024",
63	        "evidence_id": ev_id, "source_url": "", "source_title": "",
64	    }

74	    return {

87	        ],
